key_insight: A candidate phase may be thermodynamically stable on the convex hull yet still be hard to synthesize if the interfacial melt at its composition is unstable to spinodal decomposition.
engineering_relevance: Interfacial-melt stability offers a practical extra screening criterion for AI-guided materials discovery, helping prioritize phases that are not only stable but also synthesis-accessible.
method: Melt-quench molecular dynamics with a fine-tuned machine-learning interatomic potential
Analyze free-energy curvature of the interfacial melt near the target composition
Validate demixing via concentration–concentration structure factor and motif analysis
evidence_signal: In Fe–B, the ambient-pressure B-rich interfacial melt near FeB4 shows a concave free-energy landscape and structure-factor signatures of instability, while applied pressure restores convexity and matches the observed synthesis boundary.
watch_item: The descriptor depends on reliable atomistic simulations and an accurate interatomic potential, so transferability to other chemistries may require careful calibration.
paper_title: Interfacial-melt stability as a thermodynamic prerequisite for solid-state synthesis
